name: dirname
readme:
Short:        Strip last component from filename
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       Todd C. Miller (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.17

dirname - Strip last component from filename

Original author: Todd C. Miller
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE emulator with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  dirname pathname

Examples:
  dirname /usr/bin/grep    -> /usr/bin
  dirname foo/bar          -> foo
  dirname foo              -> .

Only handles / as path separator (POSIX behavior).
AmigaOS : separator in volume paths is not recognized.

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Single-file OpenBSD port (57 lines, pure C89, EASY difficulty). Key transformations: amiport/err.h for err(), amiport/stdlib.h for exit() macro, amiport/getopt.h (libnix getopt_long is broken), __progname defined directly (weak symbol stripping fix, crash-patterns), pledge()/unveil() stubbed as macros, exit(1) changed to exit(10) for AmigaOS RC conventions. Uses local POSIX-correct dirname() -- libnix dirname() modifies input in-place (crash-patterns #18), safe here because result is consumed before argv[0] is reused. Memory: CLEAN per memory audit. Performance: no findings -- program is trivially fast. 24/24 FS-UAE tests pass including Amiga-specific WORK: volume path tests.
known_limitations: Only handles / as path separator. AmigaOS paths with : (e.g., Work:data/file.txt) return . instead of Work:data. This matches POSIX behavior but differs from what an Amiga-native implementation might do.
